features.loansText: Notes Receivable from Participants Participants may borrow the lesser of $50,000 (reduced by the participant’s highest outstanding loan balance during the 12-month period ending on the date of the loan) or 50 percent of their vested account balance.

features.matchText: Other than as stated above, employer matching contributions are 100% vested after completion of one year of service.
features.vestingText: Participants become fully (100%) vested in the employer contribution upon the occurrence of death and total or permanent disability.
features.eligibilityText: Eligible participants must be employed on the last day of the Plan year and be credited with at least 501 hours of service during the year to receive an allocation of the employer profit-sharing contribution.
features.loansText: Notes Receivable from Participants Participants may borrow from their fund accounts a minimum of $500 up to a maximum equal to the lesser of $50,000 or 50 percent of their vested account balance.
features.autoEnrollText: Automatically enrolled participants have their deferral rate set at 4% of eligible compensation and their contributions invested in a Target Date Fund until changed by the participant.

features.matchText: In 2024, the Company matched 25% of the first 8% of the base compensation that a participant contributes up to the Internal Revenue Code (IRC) limitations.
features.vestingText: In the event of Plan termination, participants’ contributions remain fully vested and Company contributions, if any, become fully vested.
features.autoEscalateText: All eligible employees who are automatically enrolled in the Plan will receive an automatic increase in their deferral contribution rate of 2% of eligible compensation, to a maximum of 12%, every year on the first day of the Plan year, unless the participant selects otherwise.
features.eligibilityText: Eligible employees participate on the first day of the month which coincides with or following having reached age 21.
features.loansText: Note 8 - Notes Receivable from Participants: Participants may obtain loans from the Plan up to 50% of their vested account balance or a maximum of $50,000, whichever is less.
features.autoEnrollText: Automatic enrollment sets pre-tax deferral contribution rates at 6% of an employee’s compensation in the absence of other employee elections.

features.matchText: All active participants during the contribution period who met the continuing eligibility requirements and made eligible elective deferral contributions were eligible for a discretionary employer matching contribution.
features.vestingText: 401(k) March 28, 2024 and Vesting The participants were vested immediately in their rollover contributions, qualified matching contributions, deferral contributions and any earnings thereon.
features.rothText: Participants could have elected to contribute pre-tax and Roth deferral contributions, up to the maximum amount permitted by the Internal Revenue Code (IRC).
features.autoEnrollText: Automatically enrolled participants had their deferral rate set at 3% of eligible compensation.

features.vestingText: These participants became 100% vested in the Company contribution portion of their account as a result of this Plan termination.
features.rothText: Participants may designate all or a portion of their salary deferral contributions as Roth deferral contributions.
features.loansText: 8 National Notes receivable from participants Participants may borrow from their fund accounts a minimum of $1,000 up to a maximum equal to the lesser of $50,000 or 50% of their account balance.

features.vestingText: Participants hired before September 1, 2016, are immediately vested in employer contributions and actual earnings (losses) thereon.
features.rothText: In addition, after-tax (Roth) contributions to the Plan are permitted up to the limits allowed by law.
features.loansText: Notes Receivable from Participants - Participants may borrow from their fund accounts, subject to balance limitations.

features.rothText: Participants may also elect to make Roth contributions utilizing after-tax contributions.
features.loansText: Years of Vesting Service Percentage Less than 2 0% 2 20% 3 40% 4 60% 5 80% 6 or more 100% Notes receivable from participants – Participants may borrow from their accounts a minimum of $1,000, up to a maximum equal to the lesser of $50,000 or 50% of their vested account balance.
features.autoEnrollText: Automatically enrolled participants have their deferral rate set at 3% of eligible compensation and their contributions invested in a designated fund until elected otherwise by the participant.

features.matchText: In 2022, the Company made safe harbor matching contributions of 100% up to 6% of the participants’ voluntary contributions.
features.vestingText: Vesting Participants were immediately vested in their voluntary contributions, rollover contributions, and safe harbor matching contributions plus actual earnings thereon.
features.rothText: Contributions Eligible participants were permitted to elect to have a percentage, limited by Plan provisions, of their compensation contributed as pretax 401(k) or Roth contributions to the Plan.
